# Streak API Reference
# Source: https://streak.readme.io/reference
# Base URL: https://api.streak.com
# Authentication: HTTP Basic Auth — API key as username, empty password
# Rate limit: No hard limit; recommended max 10 requests/second

---

## OVERVIEW

The Streak API powers most functions a user can perform in Streak — creating boxes, pipelines, snippets, etc. Anything you can do in Streak can be done via the API. Exception: Gmail-connected functions (email tracking, mail merge) are unavailable.

---

## USERS

### Get Current User
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/users/me
Each Streak user has a corresponding user object. User objects cannot be altered through the API. Each API key only has privileges to access its own user object.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-current-user

### Get User
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/users/{userKey}
Returns the public details for the user specified.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-user

---

## TEAMS

### Get My Teams
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v2/teams
Get all teams the current user is a member of.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-teams

### Get a Team
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v2/teams/{teamKey}
Get a specific team by its key. The requesting user must have access to the team.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-team

---

## PIPELINES

Pipelines are a core data object representing a business process (Sales, Hiring, Bug Tracking, etc.). A pipeline defines the schema for its boxes: the set of stages and custom fields. Can be shared to users or an entire organization (orgWide=true shares to same Google Apps domain). aclEntries specifies individual user permission exceptions. The stages and fields properties are embedded in the pipeline object for convenience but must be modified via dedicated endpoints.

### List All Pipelines
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines
Returns all pipelines accessible to the authenticated user.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-pipelines

### Get a Pipeline
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}
Returns a single pipeline by key.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/getting-a-specific-pipeline

### Create a Pipeline
PUT https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines
Send url-encoded requests. Provide authorization.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-pipeline

### Update a Pipeline
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}
Edit the properties of a pipeline. To change fields or stages, use dedicated Fields/Stages endpoints. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/edit-a-pipeline

### Delete a Pipeline
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}
Delete a pipeline. Only empty pipelines (no boxes) can be deleted.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-pipeline

### Move Boxes to a Different Pipeline (Batch)
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/boxes/batch
Batch move boxes to a different pipeline. Body must be a raw JSON array (no wrapper key): [{"key":"boxKey1","boxKey":"boxKey1","pipelineKey":"newPipelineKey"},...]. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/batch-move-boxes-to-a-different-pipeline

---

## BOXES

Boxes are the rows within a pipeline. Each box has custom field values, a stage, and associated emails, tasks, comments, files, and meetings.

### List All Boxes in Pipeline
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/boxes
Get all boxes in a pipeline. Supports limit and page query params.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-boxes-in-pipeline

### Get a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}
Get a specific box by key. Response includes a contacts array of associated contacts.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-box

### Get Multiple Boxes
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes
Batch get boxes from a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-multiple-boxes

### Create a Box
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/boxes
Create a box in the specified pipeline. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-box

### Update a Box
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}
Edit box properties (name, notes, stageKey, assignees, etc.). To update field values, use the Update Field Value endpoint. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/edit-a-box

### Delete a Box
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}
Delete a box and all its associated data (files, emails, tasks).
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-box

### Get Timeline for a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/timeline
Returns the chronological timeline of events for a specific box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-timeline-for-a-box

---

## STAGES

Stages are the possible states a box can be in, defined per pipeline. All boxes in a pipeline share the same stages. To change a box's stage, update stageKey via the Update Box endpoint.

### List All Stages in Pipeline
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/stages
Lists stage definitions in a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-stages-in-pipeline

### Get a Stage
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/stages/{stageKey}
Get a specific stage definition from a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-stage

### Create a Stage
PUT https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/stages
Create a new stage in a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-stage

### Update a Stage
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/stages/{stageKey}
Edit the name of a stage.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-stage

### Delete a Stage
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/stages/{stageKey}
Delete a stage from a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-stage

---

## FIELDS

Fields are user-defined metadata on boxes, appearing as columns in the pipeline view. Defined per pipeline; all boxes share the same schema. Field types: TEXT_INPUT, DATE, TAG, FORMULA, DROPDOWN, CHECKBOX, TEAM_CONTACT.

### List All Fields in a Pipeline
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/fields
Lists field definitions in a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-fields-in-a-pipeline

### Get a Field
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Get a specific field definition.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-field

### Create a Field
PUT https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/fields
Create a new field in a pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-field

### Update a Field
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Edit a field definition. Use ?addOnly=true to append options.
Tag options: {"tagSettings":{"tags":[{"tag":"Option1"}]}}
Dropdown options: {"dropdownSettings":{"items":[{"name":"Option1"}]}}
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-field

### Delete a Field
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Delete a field. Also removes all values of this field for every box in the pipeline.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-field

### List Field Values for a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/fields
List all field values for a specific box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-field-values-for-a-box

### Get a Field Value
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Get a specific field value for a box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-field-value-for-a-box

### Update Field Value
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Update the value of a field for a box. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-field-value-for-a-box

---

## CONTACTS

Contacts represent people associated with boxes. All contacts are scoped to a team. Must have at least one of name or emailAddresses.

### Get a Contact
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/contacts/{contactKey}
Get a contact by its key.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/read-a-contact-1

### Create a Contact
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/teams/{teamKey}/contacts/
Create a contact scoped to a team. If getIfExisting is included, only emailAddresses can be passed. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-contact

### Update a Contact
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/contacts/{contactKey}
Updatable: givenName, familyName, title, other, twitterHandle, facebookHandle, linkedinHandle, photoUrl, addresses[], phoneNumbers[], emailAddresses[]. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-contact-1

### Delete a Contact
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/contacts/{contactKey}
Delete a contact by its key.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-contact

### Contacts FAQ
Get contacts from a box: GET /api/v1/boxes/{boxKey} and read the contacts array.
Search contacts by email: GET /api/v1/search?query=email@example.com — filter the contacts array.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/contacts-frequently-asked-questions

### List All Contact Fields
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/teams/{teamKey}/contactfields
List all custom fields defined for contacts in a team.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-contact-fields

### Update Contact Field Value
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/contacts/{contactKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Update a custom field value for a contact. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-contact-field-value

---

## ORGANIZATIONS

Organizations represent companies associated with boxes. All organizations are scoped to a team.

### Get an Organization
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/organizations/{organizationKey}
Read an organization by its key.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-an-organization

### Check for Existing Organizations / Create an Organization
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/teams/{teamKey}/organizations/
Create a new organization. Must have at least one of name or domains. Use getIfExisting to return existing org by domain instead of creating a duplicate. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-an-organization

### Update an Organization
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/organizations/{organizationKey}
Update an organization by its key. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-an-organization

### Delete an Organization
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/organizations/{organizationKey}
Delete an organization by its key.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-an-organization

### List All Organization Fields
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/teams/{teamKey}/organizationfields
List all custom fields defined for organizations in a team.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/list-all-organization-fields

### Update Organization Field Value
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/organizations/{organizationKey}/fields/{fieldKey}
Update a custom field value for an organization. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-organization-field-value

---

## SEARCH

### Search Boxes, Contacts, and Organizations
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/search?query={query}
Relevance-sorted search across: box name, notes, emails, text formula fields, freeform text fields. Results contain separate arrays for orgs, boxes, and contacts. Optional: pipelineKey (repeatable), stageKey (repeatable).
Example: GET /api/v1/search?query=acme&pipelineKey=abc&stageKey=5003
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/searching-for-boxes-contacts-and-organizations

### Search for a Box by Name
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/search?name={name}
Exact match search for boxes by name.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/searchng-for-boxes-by-name

---

## TASKS

Tasks create a to-do list per box. All tasks have a text property. Optionally assigned to a pipeline member with an optional due date (syncs to Google Calendar, sends email reminder).

### Get a Task
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/tasks/{taskKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/read-a-task

### Get Tasks in a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/tasks
Get all tasks associated with a box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/read-tasks-on-a-box

### Create a Task
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/tasks
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-task

### Update a Task
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/tasks/{taskKey}
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-task

### Delete a Task
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/tasks/{taskKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-task

---

## WEBHOOKS

Webhooks POST an object to a target URL when events occur. Updated objects are wrapped in an array. HTTP 410 from target causes auto-deletion. Non-200/410 responses trigger retry with backoff. Two types: pipeline-based (boxes, comments, call logs) and team-based (contacts, organizations). Admins see all webhooks; non-Admins see only their own.

### Create a Webhook
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/webhooks
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-webhook

### Update a Webhook
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v2/webhooks/{webhookKey}
Only event and targetUrl are allowed. Cannot switch between pipeline-based and team-based. Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-webhook

### Delete a Webhook
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v2/webhooks/{webhookKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-webhook

### Get All Pipeline-Based Webhooks
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/webhooks
Admins see all; non-Admins see only their own.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-all-webhooks-by-pipeline

### Get All Team-Based Webhooks
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v2/teams/{key}/webhooks
Contacts and organizations scope.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-all-webhooks-by-team

### Get a Webhook by Key
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v2/webhooks/{key}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-webhook-by-key

---

## FILES

Files are attachments associated with boxes, typically Gmail email attachments. Binary content only available for GMAIL_API type files.

### Get Files in a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/files
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-files-for-box

### Get Specific File
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/files/{fileKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-specific-file

### Get File Contents
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/files/{fileKey}/contents
Binary contents. Response mime type matches file type. GMAIL_API type only.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-file-contents

### Add File(s) to Box
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/files
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/add-files

---

## THREADS

Streak associates Gmail threads with each box in a pipeline.

### Put an Email in a Box
PUT https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/threads/{threadKey}
Associate a Gmail thread with a box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/put-an-email-in-a-box

### Get Threads in a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/threads
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-threads-in-a-box

### Get a Specific Thread
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/threads/{key}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-specific-thread

### Remove a Thread
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/threads/{key}
Disassociates thread from box (does not delete the Gmail email).
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/remove-a-thread

---

## COMMENTS

Each box can have 0 or more comments. Any user with pipeline access can create a comment.

### Get a Comment
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/comments/{commentKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-comment

### Create a Comment
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/comments
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-comment

### Update a Comment
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/comments/{commentKey}
Requires Content-Type: application/json.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-comment

### Delete a Comment
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/comments/{commentKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-comment

### Get All Comments in a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/comments
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-all-comments

---

## MEETINGS (Meeting Notes and Call Logs)

Each box can have 0 or more meeting/call log records. Any user with pipeline access can create a meeting.

### Get All Meetings in a Box
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/meetings
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-meetings-in-a-box

### Create a New Meeting
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/meetings
Requires Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-new-meeting

### Update a Meeting
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/meetings/{meetingKey}
Requires Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-meeting

### Delete a Meeting
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/meetings/{meetingKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-meeting

---

## SNIPPETS

Snippets are saved text fragments insertable into emails. Can be private or shared with a pipeline.

### Create a Snippet
PUT https://api.streak.com/api/v1/snippets
Include pipelineKey in body to share with a pipeline. Requires Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/create-a-snippet

### Update a Snippet
POST https://api.streak.com/api/v1/snippets/{snippetKey}
Requires Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/update-a-snippet

### Get All Snippets
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/snippets
Get all snippets for the authenticated user.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-all-snippets

### Get a Snippet
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/snippets/{snippetKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/get-a-specific-snippet

### Delete a Snippet
DELETE https://api.streak.com/api/v1/snippets/{snippetKey}
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/delete-a-snippet

---

## NEWSFEED

Chronological event feed of changes. All newsfeed endpoints limited to 50 most recent events.

### Newsfeed for Pipelines
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/pipelines/{pipelineKey}/newsfeed
Returns historical changes for a pipeline AND all boxes within it.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/newsfeed-for-pipelines

### Newsfeed for Boxes
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/boxes/{boxKey}/newsfeed
Returns the event history for a specific box.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/newsfeed-for-boxes

### Newsfeed for All Pipelines
GET https://api.streak.com/api/v1/newsfeed
Returns the newsfeed across all pipelines accessible to the authenticated user.
Docs: https://streak.readme.io/reference/newsfeed-for-all-pipelines

---

## AUTHENTICATION

HTTP Basic Auth. API key as username, empty password.
curl example: curl https://api.streak.com/api/v1/users/me -u YOUR_API_KEY:
To get API key: Gmail > Streak sidebar icon > Integrations > Streak API > Create New Key.

---

## API VERSIONING

v1: Most endpoints
v2: Boxes (create), webhooks, teams, contacts (create), organizations (create)

---

## GENERAL NOTES

- Stages and fields are read-only in the pipeline object; use dedicated endpoints to modify them.
- Contacts and organizations are always scoped to a team; teamKey required for creation.
- File binary content retrieval only available for GMAIL_API type files.
- Removing a thread disassociates it from the box but does not delete the Gmail email.
- Webhook HTTP 410 response from target causes auto-deletion; non-200 triggers retry with backoff.
- Search simultaneously covers boxes, contacts, and orgs by name, notes, emails, and text fields.
- Batch pipeline move: JSON body must be a raw array, not wrapped in an object key.
